CRITICAL_NEAR_MISS.pert_said: CLOUD VOLUME: ATTACHED / NOT YET MOUNTED; work package J = FORMAT APPROVED
CRITICAL_NEAR_MISS.reality: /dev/sdc was ALREADY ext4-formatted, ALREADY mounted at /var/lib/helm, holding 31GB
CRITICAL_NEAR_MISS.consequence_if_executed: mkfs on /dev/sdc would have DESTROYED 31GB (19G containerd + 13G docker + postgres)
CRITICAL_NEAR_MISS.why_it_did_not_happen: pre-format identity guard inspected the device read-only before any write
CRITICAL_NEAR_MISS.lesson: 'FORMAT APPROVED' was granted against a stale picture of the world. Approval authorizes an action, it does not verify the precondition. The guard must run anyway.

persistence_proof.method: stop docker/containerd -> real umount -> mount from /etc/fstab (no device arg) -> hash compare
persistence_proof.bare_dir_significance: 0 entries proves the mount was real, not a coincidental directory
persistence_proof.du_delta_31G_to_22G: EXPLAINED (not data loss): first du ran with docker up and counted overlayfs mounts; second ran with docker stopped. Canary hash identical proves integrity. Flagged, not waved off.

first_false_green_caught: My initial remount test reported PASS while the umount had actually FAILED (target busy) — the hashes matched only because the volume was never unmounted. Vacuous green. Re-run with captured evidence.

split_brain_assessment.relay_runs: helm-api-mirror (uvicorn helm_live_api), prometheus, postgres, redis, grafana, ollama, backup-agent
split_brain_assessment.enabled_systemd_units_for_helm: NONE (a reboot will not start a competing scheduler)
split_brain_assessment.port_8770: bound 0.0.0.0 via docker-proxy BUT ufw active; external probe from Mac -> HTTP 000 (unreachable)
split_brain_assessment.verdict: NO SPLIT-BRAIN. Relay is a passive mirror with no authority and no scheduler.

remaining_for_K_cutover: install single authority pointer on relay
prove HELM runs with MacBook OFF
